name: Lemon Couscous Cake
recipeIngredient: Finely grated zest of one lemon (or to taste)
1 tablespoon kudzu or arrowroot powder dissolved in 1 tablespoon apple juice
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large saucepan, heat the apple juice to boiling. Add dates and salt.
Reduce heat and simmer gently for 10 minutes.
2. While the dates are simmering, heat a large heavy-bottomed skillet over medium-low heat.
Add couscous and cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly toasted (5-10 minutes).
3. Gradually add the toasted couscous to the simmering apple juice and dates, stirring constantly.
Cook for 3 to 4 minutes, until the mixture thickens. Stir in lemon zest and remove from heat.
4. Rinse a 10-inch round or 9-inch square glass or ceramic baking pan with water. Shake out excess water; do not dry.
Spread the couscous mixture evenly in the pan and set aside to cool.
5. In a small saucepan, whisk apple juice and maple syrup until just simmering.
Add dissolved kudzu (or arrowroot powder) and whisk constantly until thickened.
Pour the topping over the cooled couscous.
